identifier: Advances in Cryptology — CRYPTO '87, LNCS 293, pp. 369-378; doi:10.1007/3-540-48184-2_32
summary: Introduces tree authentication, now known as the Merkle tree: a binary tree of hash values whose root commits to a set of leaf values, so that any single leaf can be authenticated against the root using only a logarithmic-length path of sibling hashes. This is the foundational construction for inclusion proofs verified against a committed root.
title: A Digital Signature Based on a Conventional Encryption Function
